key_insight: Machine-learning Monte Carlo/molecular-dynamics simulations show that Al content controls whether (MoCrTi) alloys undergo one or two chemical order-disorder transitions, with B2-type ordering strongly tied to composition.
engineering_relevance: Chemical ordering can substantially increase stiffness and produce a non-monotonic strength/stiffness trend, helping identify compositions such as Mo30Cr30Ti30Al10 for high-temperature structural design.
method: Temperature-dependent heat capacity and short-range order analysis
evidence_signal: The abstract reports simulated thermodynamic and mechanical trends across multiple compositions, including heat-capacity peaks, short-range order parameters, and elastic constants/moduli.
watch_item: Results depend on the accuracy and transferability of the trained interatomic potential, so predictions should be validated against experiments or higher-fidelity calculations.
paper_title: Study of ordering in (MoCrTi)$_{100-x}$Al$_x$ refractory high-entropy alloys using machine learning interatomic potential
